hindiTranslation: वे आशाकी सैकड़ों फाँसियोंसे बँधे हुए मनुष्य कामक्रोधके परायण होकर पदार्थोंका भोग करनेके लिये अन्यायपूर्वक धनसंचय करनेकी चेष्टा करते रहते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Bound by hundreds of cords of hope, driven by desire and anger, they strive to gather wealth unjustly for the enjoyment of desires.
essence: Desire and anger turn ambition into injustice.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Endless hopes trap people. Desire and anger drive them to gather wealth by unfair means just to feed more wanting.
simpleInsight: You can see how craving multiplies itself until fairness no longer matters.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, while Arjuna stands frozen, Krishna describes the inner life of those ruled by the lower nature: trapped by countless hopes, pushed by craving and rage, they chase wealth through injustice. This verse shows the behavior behind that blindness.
modernRelevance: A person cuts corners, hides numbers, and calls it ambition. The hunger for more has already turned into a cage.
translationLiteral: आशापाशशतैः (by hundreds of cords of hope) / बद्धाः (bound) / कामक्रोधपरायणाः (devoted to desire and anger) / ईहन्ते (they strive) / कामभोगार्थम् (for the sake of enjoyment of desire) / अन्यायेन (unjustly) / अर्थसञ्चयान् (accumulations of wealth)
